mn39:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Aṅgans at Assapura, a town of the Aṅgans.
mn39:10.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:10.10: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:10.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘We will devote ourselves to wakefulness. By day, while walking and sitting, we will cleanse mind [*cittaṁ*] of obstructive *dhammas*.
mn39:10.3: In the first watch of the night, while walking and sitting, we will cleanse mind [*cittaṁ*] of obstructive *dhammas*.
mn39:10.4: In the middle watch of the night, we will lie down on the right side in the lion’s posture, placing one foot on the other, with *sati* and full awareness [*sampajāno*], attending to the perception of rising up.
mn39:10.5: In the last watch of the night, after rising, while walking and sitting, we will cleanse mind [*cittaṁ*] of obstructive *dhammas*.’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:10.6: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:10.7: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, our verbal conduct is purified, our mental conduct is purified, our livelihood is purified, we guard the doors of the *indriyas*, we are moderate in eating, and we are devoted to wakefulness.
mn39:10.8: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:10.9: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:11.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:11.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘We will be endowed with *sati* and full awareness [*sampajaññena*]: acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when going forward and returning; acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when looking ahead and looking away; acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when bending and stretching; acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when wearing robe, bowl, and outer robe; acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when eating, drinking, chewing, and tasting; acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when defecating and urinating; acting with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when walking, standing, sitting, sleeping, waking, speaking, and keeping silent.’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:11.3: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:11.4: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, our verbal conduct is purified, our mental conduct is purified, our livelihood is purified, we guard the doors of the *indriyas*, we are moderate in eating, we are devoted to wakefulness, and we are endowed with *sati* and full awareness [*satisampajaññena*].
mn39:11.5: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:11.6: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:11.7: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:12.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:12.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* resorts to a secluded lodging—a wilderness, the root of a tree, a mountain, a ravine, a hillside cave, a charnel ground, a forest thicket, the open air, or a heap of straw.
mn39:13.1: After the meal, on returning from the almsround, he sits down, having folded his legs crosswise, set his body erect, and established *sati* before him.
mn39:13.2: Abandoning desire for the world, he dwells with a mind [*cetasā*] free from desire, and he purifies mind [*cittaṁ*] of desire.
mn39:13.3: Abandoning ill will and hostility, he dwells with a mind [*citta*] free from ill will, compassionate for the welfare of all breathing beings, and he purifies mind [*cittaṁ*] of ill will and hostility.
mn39:13.4: Abandoning dullness and drowsiness, he dwells free from dullness and drowsiness, perceiving light, with *sati* and full awareness [*sampajāno*], and he purifies mind [*cittaṁ*] of dullness and drowsiness.
mn39:13.5: Abandoning restlessness and remorse, he dwells unagitated, with mind [*citta*] inwardly stilled, and he purifies mind [*cittaṁ*] of restlessness and remorse.
mn39:13.6: Abandoning doubt, he dwells having gone beyond doubt, free of uncertainty regarding skillful *dhammas*, and he purifies mind [*cittaṁ*] of doubt.
mn39:14.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a person were to take a loan and undertake a business.
mn39:14.11: ‘Formerly I was ill, afflicted, gravely sick; food did not agree with me, and my body had no strength. But now I am recovered from that illness; food agrees with me, and my body has strength.’
mn39:14.12: Because of that, he would gain gladness and experience happiness.
mn39:14.13: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a person were imprisoned in a jail.
mn39:14.14: After some time he would be released from that imprisonment, safe and secure, and without any loss to his property.
mn39:14.16: ‘Formerly I was imprisoned in a jail. But now I am released from that imprisonment, safe and secure, and there is no loss at all to my property.’
mn39:14.17: Because of that, he would gain gladness and experience happiness.
mn39:14.18: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a person were a slave, not his own master, dependent on another, unable to go where he wished.
mn39:14.19: After some time he would be freed from that slavery, his own master, not dependent on another, a free man able to go where he wished.
mn39:14.2: And those business affairs of his were to succeed.
mn39:14.21: ‘Formerly I was a slave, not my own master, dependent on another, unable to go where I wished. But now I am freed from that slavery, my own master, not dependent on another, a free man able to go where I wish.’
mn39:14.22: Because of that, he would gain gladness and experience happiness.
mn39:14.23: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a person, prosperous and wealthy, were to set out on a road through a wilderness.
mn39:14.24: After some time he would emerge from that wilderness safe and secure, and without any loss to his property.
mn39:14.26: ‘Formerly, prosperous and wealthy, I set out on a road through a wilderness.
mn39:14.27: Now I have emerged from that wilderness safe and secure, and there is no loss at all to my property.’
mn39:14.28: Because of that, he would gain gladness and experience happiness.
mn39:14.29: Just so, *bhikkhus*, as debt, disease, imprisonment, slavery, and a wilderness road, a *bhikkhu* regards these five hindrances as not abandoned in himself.
mn39:14.3: He would pay off those old debts that were the principal, and there would still be something left over for supporting a wife.
mn39:14.30: And just as freedom from debt, health, release from prison, freedom, and safe ground, so a *bhikkhu* regards these five hindrances as abandoned in himself.
mn39:14.5: ‘Formerly I took a loan and undertook a business, and those business affairs of mine succeeded.
mn39:14.6: So I paid off those old debts that were the principal, and there is still something left over for supporting a wife.’
mn39:14.7: Because of that, he would gain gladness and experience happiness.
mn39:14.8: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a person were ill, afflicted, gravely sick; food would not agree with him, and his body would have no strength.
mn39:14.9: After some time he would recover from that illness; food would agree with him, and his body would have strength.
mn39:15.1: Having abandoned these five hindrances, defilements of mind [*cetaso*] that weaken discernment [*paññāya*],
mn39:15.2: quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful *dhammas*, he enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
mn39:15.3: He drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with the *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by the *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
mn39:15.4: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a skilled bath attendant or bath attendant’s apprentice might pour bath powder into a bronze basin and knead it, sprinkling it again and again with water, so that the ball of bath powder would be soaked with moisture, saturated throughout inside and out, yet not dripping;
mn39:15.5: just so, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with the *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by the *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
mn39:16.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the stilling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], he enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, which is inner serenity and unification [*ekodibhāvaṁ*] of mind [*cetaso*], without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijaṁ*].
mn39:16.2: He drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with the *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijena*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by the *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijena*].
mn39:16.3: Just as, *bhikkhus*, there were a lake whose waters welled up from below. It would have no inflow of water from the eastern direction, no inflow of water from the western direction, no inflow of water from the northern direction, and no inflow of water from the southern direction, and the sky would not from time to time send down proper showers of rain. Yet from that very lake a cool spring of water would well up and drench, steep, fill, and pervade that very lake with cool water, so that there would be no part of the whole lake not pervaded by cool water.
mn39:16.4: Just so, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with the *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijena*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by the *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijena*].
mn39:17.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the fading away of *pīti*, he dwells equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* and full awareness [*sampajāno*], and experiences *sukha* with the body. He enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*, of which the noble ones say: ‘Equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati*, he dwells in *sukha*.’
mn39:17.2: He drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *sukha* devoid of *pīti* [*nippītikena*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by *sukha* devoid of *pīti* [*nippītikena*].
mn39:17.3: Just as, *bhikkhus*, in a pond of blue lotuses, or of red lotuses, or of white lotuses, some blue lotuses, red lotuses, or white lotuses, born in the water, grown in the water, not rising up above the water, would thrive immersed in the water; from tip to root they would be drenched, steeped, filled, and pervaded with cool water, so that there would be no part of those blue lotuses, red lotuses, or white lotuses not pervaded by cool water.
mn39:17.4: Just so, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* drenches, steeps, fills, and pervades this very body with *sukha* devoid of *pīti* [*nippītikena*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by *sukha* devoid of *pīti* [*nippītikena*].
mn39:18.1: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the abandoning of *sukha* and the abandoning of *dukkha*, and with the earlier disappearance of joy and dejection, he enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither painful nor pleasant, and has purity of equanimity [*upekkhā*] and *sati* [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*].
mn39:18.2: He sits pervading this very body with a purified, bright mind [*cetasā*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by a purified, bright mind [*cetasā*].
mn39:18.3: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a person might sit wrapped from head down in a white cloth, so that there would be no part of his whole body not pervaded by the white cloth.
mn39:18.4: Just so, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* sits pervading this very body with a purified, bright mind [*cetasā*], so that there is no part of his whole body not pervaded by a purified, bright mind [*cetasā*].
mn39:19.1: When his mind [*citte*] is thus endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhite*], purified, bright, unblemished, rid of defilements, pliant, workable, steady, and attained to imperturbability, he directs mind [*cittaṁ*] to the knowledge of recollecting former lives.
mn39:19.2: He recollects his many kinds of former lives, that is: one birth, two births … Thus with their aspects and details he recollects his many kinds of former lives.
mn39:19.3: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a person might go from his own village to another village, and from that village to yet another village, and then from that village might return to his own village. It would occur to him: ‘I went from my own village to that village. There I stood thus, sat thus, spoke thus, kept silent thus. From that village I went to that other village. There too I stood thus, sat thus, spoke thus, kept silent thus. And now from that village I have returned to my own village.’
mn39:19.4: Just so, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* recollects his many kinds of former lives, that is: one birth, two births … Thus with their aspects and details he recollects his many kinds of former lives.
mn39:2.2: And when you are asked, ‘Who are you?’ you declare, ‘We are ascetics.’
mn39:2.3: Since you are thus designated, *bhikkhus*, and thus profess, you should conduct yourselves having undertaken those *dhammas* that make one an ascetic and make one a brahmin, thinking: ‘Thus this designation of ours will be true, and this profession genuine.
mn39:2.4: And for those whose robe, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and medical requisites we make use of, those acts of support toward us will become of great fruit and great benefit; and this going forth of ours will not be barren, but fruitful and fertile.’
mn39:2.5: That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:20.1: When his mind [*citte*] is thus endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhite*], purified, bright, unblemished, rid of defilements, pliant, workable, steady, and attained to imperturbability, he directs mind [*cittaṁ*] to the knowledge of the passing away and reappearance of beings.
mn39:20.2: With the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, he sees beings passing away and reappearing, inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, in a good destination and a bad destination; he understands beings as they fare according to their kamma …
mn39:20.3: Just as, *bhikkhus*, there were two houses with doors, and a person with good eyesight standing between them might see people entering a house, coming out, walking back and forth, and moving about.
mn39:20.4: Just so, *bhikkhus*, with the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, a *bhikkhu* sees beings passing away and reappearing, inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, in a good destination and a bad destination; he understands beings as they fare according to their kamma …
mn39:21.1: When his mind [*citte*] is thus endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhite*], purified, bright, unblemished, rid of defilements, pliant, workable, steady, and attained to imperturbability, he directs mind [*cittaṁ*] to the knowledge of the ending of the *āsavas*.
mn39:21.10: ‘This lake of water is clear, serene, and untroubled. And here these shellfish and snails, gravel and pebbles, and shoals of fish are moving about and staying still.’
mn39:21.11: Just so, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* understands as it truly is: ‘This is *dukkha*’ …
mn39:21.12: He understands: ‘There is no more of this state of being.’
mn39:21.2: He understands as it truly is: ‘This is *dukkha*.’ He understands as it truly is: ‘This is the arising of *dukkha*.’ He understands as it truly is: ‘This is the cessation of *dukkha*.’ He understands as it truly is: ‘This is the path leading to the cessation of *dukkha*.’
mn39:21.3: He understands as it truly is: ‘These are the *āsavas*.’ He understands as it truly is: ‘This is the arising of the *āsavas*.’ He understands as it truly is: ‘This is the cessation of the *āsavas*.’ He understands as it truly is: ‘This is the path leading to the cessation of the *āsavas*.’
mn39:21.4: For one knowing thus, seeing thus, mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated from the sensuality *āsava* [*kāmāsavāpi*], mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated from the *bhava* *āsava* [*bhavāsavāpi*], and mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated from the blindness [*avijjā*] *āsava* [*avijjāsavāpi*].
mn39:21.5: When it is liberated, there comes the knowledge: ‘It is liberated.’
mn39:21.6: He understands: ‘Birth is ended, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.’
mn39:21.7: Just as, *bhikkhus*, in a mountain cleft there were a lake of water, clear, serene, and untroubled.
mn39:21.8: There a person with good eyesight, standing on the bank, might see shellfish and snails, gravel and pebbles, and shoals of fish moving about and staying still.
mn39:22.1: This, *bhikkhus*, is what is called a *bhikkhu* who is an ‘ascetic’, a ‘brahmin’, a ‘bathed one’, a ‘knower’, a ‘purged one’, a ‘noble one’, and an ‘*arahant*’.
mn39:23.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* an ascetic?
mn39:23.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are stilled—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:23.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is an ascetic, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:24.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* a brahmin?
mn39:24.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are driven out—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:24.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is a brahmin, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:25.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* a bathed one?
mn39:25.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are washed away—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:25.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is a bathed one, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:26.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* a knower?
mn39:26.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are known—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:26.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is a knower, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:27.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* a purged one?
mn39:27.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are flushed out—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:27.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is a purged one, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:28.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* a noble one?
mn39:28.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are far away—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:28.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is a noble one, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:29.2: In him bad unskillful *dhammas* are far away—defiling, leading to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, old age, and death.
mn39:29.3: That is how a *bhikkhu* is an *arahant*, *bhikkhus*.”
mn39:29.5: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* rejoiced in what the *Bhagavā* had said.
mn39:29.6: The Greater Discourse at Assapura is finished, the ninth.
mn39:3.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, are the *dhammas* that make one an ascetic and make one a brahmin?
mn39:3.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘We will be endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing.’
mn39:3.3: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:3.4: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing. That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:3.5: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:3.6: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:4.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:4.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘Our bodily conduct will be purified—straightforward, open, not deceptive, and restrained.
mn39:4.3: And because of that purity of bodily conduct, we will neither exalt ourselves nor disparage others.’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:4.4: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:4.5: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, and our bodily conduct is purified.
mn39:4.6: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:4.7: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:4.8: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:5.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:5.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘Our verbal conduct will be purified—straightforward, open, not deceptive, and restrained.
mn39:5.3: And because of that purity of verbal conduct, we will neither exalt ourselves nor disparage others.’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:5.4: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:5.5: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, and our verbal conduct is purified.
mn39:5.6: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:5.7: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:5.8: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:6.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:6.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘Our mental conduct will be purified—straightforward, open, not deceptive, and restrained.
mn39:6.3: And because of that purity of mental conduct, we will neither exalt ourselves nor disparage others.’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:6.4: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:6.5: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, our verbal conduct is purified, and our mental conduct is purified.
mn39:6.6: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:6.7: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:6.8: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:7.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:7.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘Our livelihood will be purified—straightforward, open, not deceptive, and restrained.
mn39:7.3: And because of that purity of livelihood, we will neither exalt ourselves nor disparage others.’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:7.4: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:7.5: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, our verbal conduct is purified, our mental conduct is purified, and our livelihood is purified.
mn39:7.6: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:7.7: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:7.8: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:8.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:8.10: Since, if one dwells with the mind *indriya* [*manindriyaṁ*] unguarded, bad unskillful *dhammas* of desire and dejection might flood in, we will practice for its restraint, we will guard the mind *indriya* [*manindriyaṁ*], we will undertake restraint regarding the mind *indriya* [*manindriye*].’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:8.11: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:8.12: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, our verbal conduct is purified, our mental conduct is purified, our livelihood is purified, and we guard the doors of the *indriyas*.
mn39:8.13: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:8.14: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:8.15: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
mn39:8.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘We will guard the doors of the *indriyas*.
mn39:8.3: Having seen a form with the eye, we will not grasp at the *nimitta* or the details.
mn39:8.4: Since, if one dwells with the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriyaṁ*] unguarded, bad unskillful *dhammas* of desire and dejection might flood in, we will practice for its restraint, we will guard the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriyaṁ*], we will undertake restraint regarding the eye *indriya* [*cakkhundriye*].
mn39:8.5: Having heard a sound with the ear …
mn39:8.6: Having smelled an odor with the nose …
mn39:8.7: Having tasted a flavor with the tongue …
mn39:8.8: Having touched a tangible with the body …
mn39:8.9: Having cognized a *dhamma* with the mind, we will not grasp at the *nimitta* or the details.
mn39:9.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is still to be done?
mn39:9.2: You should train yourselves thus: ‘We will be moderate in eating; reflecting appropriately, we will take food
mn39:9.3: not for amusement, not for intoxication, not for fattening, not for beautification, but only for the endurance and continuance of this body, for the ending of harm, and for supporting the holy life, thinking: “Thus we will put an end to old *vedanā* and will not produce new *vedanā*; and we will live blamelessly and at ease.”’ That is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*.
mn39:9.4: But, *bhikkhus*, it might occur to you:
mn39:9.5: ‘We are endowed with conscience and fear of wrongdoing, our bodily conduct is purified, our verbal conduct is purified, our mental conduct is purified, our livelihood is purified, we guard the doors of the *indriyas*, and we are moderate in eating.
mn39:9.6: That much is enough; so much has been done. We have attained the goal of the ascetic life; there is nothing further for us to do.’ And you might be satisfied with just that.
mn39:9.7: I tell you, *bhikkhus*, I declare to you, *bhikkhus*:
mn39:9.8: ‘Do not let the goal of the ascetic life slip away from you while there is still more to be done, you who seek the goal of the ascetic life.’
